methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $65M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Peak Energy, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Landon Mossburg, has raised $65M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of four same-stage, same-sector private peers (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) alongside one public comparable, yielding a dataset of five primary benchmarks with valuations ranging from $110M to $1.8B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using the $65M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a $130M valuation. The peer-median method across the four private comparables yields a $175M implied valuation, with peers clustering at $110M, $150M, $200M, and $1.8B respectively—the latter representing significant outlier performance. The median across all four peers is $175M; the range spans $110M to $1.8B.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $110M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $152M. Key uncertainties center on the wide dispersion within the peer set—the $1.8B Watershed valuation suggests high outcome variance in climate tech—and the limited number of comparable financings. The $152M median represents a ~2.3x step-up from total raised, broadly consistent with stage-typical progression.
